title: ruscus hypoglossum subsp. microglossus
intro: Ruscus hypoglossum subsp. microglossus, also known as the Dwarf Ruscus or Dwarf Butcher's Broom, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and supporting the immune system, making it a potential remedy for conditions like arthritis, varicose veins, and wounds. Ruscus hypoglossum subsp. microglossus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
